108.status: Manager Kurt Suzuki said Pomeranz’s placement on the IL was the Angels being “cautious.” Pomeranz brought it to the team's attention after Tuesday’s game.

Went 1-for-3 with two walks at Triple-A Salt Lake City on Tuesday after appearing in the Arizona Complex League on May 11. Suzuki said the Angels will discuss next steps with him after his Triple-A start on Wednesday.
Began rehab assignment with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on May 10 and struck out 11 batters in 4 2/3 innings. Suzuki said that Rodriguez making his next appearance with the Angels is “on the table.”
Made three appearances for Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on rehab assignment. Scratched May 10 after he didn’t bounce back as anticipated. Manager Kurt Suzuki is not concerned rehab will be affected.
Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself while trying to beat out an infield single. Requires further evaluation after testing revealed plantar fasciitis.
Left his outing before the start of the third inning on April 29. Underwent an MRI, will be shut down for 3-4 weeks before starting a throwing program.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Scratched from lineup ahead of May 12 game vs. the Yankees after feeling discomfort during pregame work. Underwent an MRI and was placed on IL the next day.
Shut down from baseball activities on May 4 after experiencing discomfort during throwing progression. Met with Dr. Neal ElAttrache on May 11. Still weighing options and next steps.
Began third rehab assignment on May 7 and went 0-for-6 in three games for Double-A Chesapeake. Moved to Triple-A Norfolk on May 12 and went 0-for-4 while starting at third base.
Began rehab assignment with Double-A Chesapeake on May 9 and went 1-for-6 in two games. Moved to Triple-A Norfolk and went 2-for-4 with an RBI double.
Had not pitched since May 6 in Miami. Went on injured list after not appearing in five consecutive games.
Imaging came back clean. Taking anti-inflammatory medication. Began treatment May 4. Not yet throwing as of May 11, though expected to do so in near future.
Exited May 7 start in Miami due to discomfort in left forearm. Could receive cortisone shot, though other treatment options remain on table.
Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Expected to miss at least several weeks.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return.

111.status: Took an important step on May 13, throwing a 20-pitch bullpen session, marking his first trip to the mound since he went on the injured list.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Making 75 throws out to 105 feet three days a week, and 30 throws at 60 feet three days a week while throwing changeups those days. Targeting mound work in late June.
Has been playing catch out to 90 feet and will continue to increase volume of throws and distance as he goes.

111.expectedReturn: First day he is eligible to return on May 17
111.status: Threw another bullpen session on May 12 and hopes to return when first eligible on May 17.
Playing catch out to 90 feet and hoping to throw off mound by end of week.
Expected to visit a doctor soon to gauge if any progress is being made.

111.expectedReturn: No earlier than mid to late June
111.status: Will soon have consultation with Dr. Keith Meister to get better idea of where he's at.

112.status: Threw live on May 13 and everything went well. The Cubs will schedule Thielbar's rehab appearance this weekend.
Threw 81 pitches in four shutout innings (four strikeouts, four walks and two hits allowed) in fifth rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on May 9.
Had a setback after bullpen session on May 7, per manager Craig Counsell. Dealing with stress reaction near triceps/shoulder. Shut down for a month and has moved rehab to Arizona.
Injured left knee in fluke accident at home on May 6. Had successful left knee meniscectomy surgery on May 7 in Chicago. Expected to miss roughly six weeks.

Felt discomfort in recent bullpen session. Checkup with Dr. Keith Meister (who did Steele's UCL revision repair on April 18, 2025) showed a flexor strain. Shut down from throwing until asymptomatic, and will be rechecked in a month.
Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing and did not recover well. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 1 prospect, No. 50 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Moved to Minor League IL out of caution. Has resumed playing catch, per president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Allowed five runs, including one homer, on seven hits and a walk in one-plus inning May 12 for Triple-A Louisville against Indianapolis.
MRI exam on May 8 showed no structural damage.

113.expectedReturn: May 17 for his next start vs. Cleveland
113.status: X-rays came back negative after taking a Daylen Lile 106.7 mph liner off the outside of his right foot. Manager Terry Francona said the team is "hopeful" Singer will make his next start.
Throwing out to 150 feet in Arizona. Manager Terry Francona said on May 12 that Greene's first bullpen session is expected to happen around May 22.
Took swings in the batting cage May 12 off a tee. Will have an MRI on May 14 with the hope of traveling with the team to Cleveland on May 15.
MRI exam showed a Grade 2 strain, with estimated time away to be four to eight weeks. Starting throwing from his knees on May 8.
Went to Goodyear, Ariz., to begin rehab process on May 5. Moved to the 60-day IL on May 6, but there was no setback.

114.status: Made first Minor League rehab appearance on May 12. Threw 19 pitches (11 strikes) over one inning with Double-A Akron.
Returned from rehab assignment due to viral illness that caused him not to pitch since April 24. Will begin new rehab stint once back to full strength.
Manager Stephen Vogt noted on May 8 that Arias' rehab is "going to be a little bit longer. He hit a little snag." Was initially expected to miss four to eight weeks.

115.status: Threw 35-40 pitches while facing hitters in Pittsburgh on May 13 and is scheduled to start a rehab assignment with Double-A Hartford on May 19.
Will make his next rehab appearance with Triple-A Albuquerque after he made two appearances each with High-A Spokane and Double-A Hartford.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continues playing catch at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Tossed three perfect innings in second rehab start for Single-A Lakeland on May 8. Sent on rehab assignment to Triple-A Toledo on May 13.
Threw a bullpen session on May 8 and is scheduled for another May 13. Not expected to need rehab assignment before MLB return.
Left game May 9 after crashing into the wall chasing down a Bobby Witt Jr. inside-the-park home run. Diagnosed with AC joint sprain but no structural damage based on tests.
Transferred to 60-day IL on May 10. Threw a two-inning, 38-pitch simulated game on May 9 in Kansas City. Will throw another, longer sim game next weekend in Detroit.
Tossed a hitless inning with a walk and no strikeouts in second rehab start for Single-A Lakeland May 9. Threw 16 pitches, nine strikes. Fastball averaged 95.4 miles per hour, topped out at 96.4.
Underwent surgery May 6 to remove loose body from elbow. Will report to Lakeland for initial stages of rehab, but will not throw for at least the next few days.
Received cortisone injection May 8 to remove remaining inflammation in forearm. Will resume throwing soon.
Threw latest bullpen session on May 8.
Tossed a scoreless inning on one hit with two strikeouts in his first rehab appearance for Single-A Lakeland on May 8.
Left May 2 game with discomfort after sliding into home plate. Tests showed no structural damage, but he is still feeling some soreness.
Completing rehab daily after receiving a cortisone injection in mid-April.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9.

117.status: Held out of second Minor League rehab game May 13 with a sore neck. Took a nasty tumble in collision with a runner May 12 at Double-A Corpus Christi. Went 1-for-2 with a double and a hit-by-pitch.
Pearson threw a 1-2-3 sixth inning in a Minor League rehab outing May 12 for Triple-A Sugar Land, topping out at 101.3 mph.
Threw his second bullpen May 12 and is scheduled throw another one May 15.
Threw one one inning (16 pitches), striking out one and allowing one hit, in a Minor League rehab outing May 12 with Double-A Corpus Christi.
Went 0-for-4 in his first Minor League rehab assignment game May 12 with Double-A Corpus Christi.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. He will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.
Continuing to progress in his throwing progression and baseball activity.
Experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8. Sent home to Houston to see doctors.
Trammell is running, hitting and throwing and could be nearing a Minor League rehab assignment.
Was scratched prior to the May 4 game against the Dodgers after suffering an injury swinging the bat in BP and placed on IL.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Continuing to go through catch progression, with May 13 session going well, according to manager Matt Quatraro.
Threw 42 pitches (31 strikes) in 2 1/3 scoreless innings in eighth rehab appearance for Triple-A Omaha.
Suffered injury during first rehab outing on May 6 and will be shut down for three weeks. Returned from rehab assignment May 8.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 season with an elbow injury, has continued to have problems with soreness. Scheduled to see a specialist to determine next steps.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Had surgery on his elbow on April 22. He thinks he will be able to start playing catch a couple weeks after the stitches are removed from his elbow.
Aggravated the bone spur while covering first base on May 8. He is expected to miss at least three weeks.
Exited his May 6 start after one inning with lower back pain. An MRI revealed "nothing really significant," per manager Dave Roberts.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Began a Minor League rehab assignment on May 2. He will likely take close to the maximum 30 days allowed as he builds up his arm.
Tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen on March 13. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in his shoulder. Has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training ramp-up ahead.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Progressed to throwing off the mound by the end of Spring Training, but the team is building him up slowly.
Started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Signed to a one-year Major League deal with a club option for the 2027 season on May 5. Reported to West Palm Beach training facility on May 7.
Played catch at a distance of 90 feet, threw off the mound (15 pitches). Will continue throwing progression.
Threw another 30-pitch bullpen session on May 6 at the Nationals’ training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Threw a 30-pitch bullpen session on May 6.
Threw a 25-pitch bullpen session on May 6.
Played catch on May 2, will continue throwing progression at Nationals' training complex.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Underwent Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on April 20 to repair flexor tendon and UCL..

121.status: Tore his meniscus on a swing in a May 12 game against the Tigers. Will undergo surgery as soon as possible and miss 6-8 weeks.

121.injury: Left lat surgery, left hip discomfort
121.status: Threw a second bullpen session on May 12 and expects to return to game action the weekend of May 16. Will not require a full 30-day buildup but is still 2-3 weeks away from the Majors, per president of baseball operations David Stearns.

Received an injection April 30 but was still experiencing symptoms as of a May 12 update. Is consulting with outside doctors, though surgery is not on the table. Timeline will remain fluid until symptoms subside.

Wrist soreness has improved, but bursitis continues to persist. Continues to participate in baseball activities but experiences “some good days, and some other days when it's not as good.” Will remain in a holding pattern until that changes.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Undergoing further testing to determine the severity of his shoulder injury, which occurred during his outing on May 12. No timetable for return.
MRI on May 11 confirmed subluxation. Will begin rehab to strengthen and stabilize shoulder before reintroducing baseball activity. No timetable for return.

133.injury: Mid foot bone bruise in right foot
133.status: Continuing baseball activities, including on-field batting practice and outfield drills. No timetable for return.
Still being held out of all baseball activities at this time. No timetable for return.
Began a throwing progression April 19. No timetable for his return.

134.status: Pitched four scoreless innings in rehab start with Double-A Altoona on May 12. Allowed three hits, struck out four and walked none while throwing 36 of 52 pitches for strikes.
Sustained apparent injury on May 9 in San Francisco. Foot began swelling during flight to Pittsburgh on May 10. Pirates are still evaluating the seriousness of the infection.
Felt slight tugging in his left leg prior to the Pirates' game on May 7. Injury does not appear to be serious.
Placed on the 15-day injured list after serving a two-game MLB suspension for throwing at Cincinnati’s Sal Stewart in a May 2 game.

135.status: Landed on the IL after his start on May 1. Slated to begin a throwing program in mid-May after two weeks of rest and recovery.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Began a rehab stint on May 7. Is building up as a starter.

Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Has played light catch but has recently been shut down entirely.
Experienced concussion symptoms after a fastball off the chin on April 18. Initially cleared protocols, but symptoms arose in the days afterward. Has not resumed baseball activity.
Fouled a ball off his toe on May 5, sustaining what the team said was a small fracture. No clear timeline for a return, but absence is not expected to be extensive.

Exited April 12 start with elbow stiffness; shut down from throwing. MRI revealed flexor strain. Expected to pitch again this season, though manager Craig Stammen noted Pivetta's absence would last "weeks and maybe months."
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Set to come off the injured list and make start in Houston on May 13. Made his fourth rehab start on May 6 at High-A Everett and threw 61 pitches.More »
Played catch for the first time since the issue first surfaced, on May 5. MRI revealed "trace inflammation" but no structural damage.More »
Began rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on May 5. Suffered the injury during brief callup from April 14-16 in San Diego.
Placed on injured list on May 4, five days after his most recent appearance.
Began a rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on May 3, which will at minimum last seven days. Final hurdle to clear is throwing to the plate, along with live at-bats.
Placed on the IL before May 2 game vs. Royals.
Injured the other (left) groin during a rehab assignment and was shut down on April 18. Will be reassessed in mid-to-late May to begin a fresh rehab assignment.
Experienced setback during early stages of throwing program and will now have to begin fresh once he's cleared to throw again.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 7 and could be activated during the Giants' series against the A's from May 15-17.
Began a rehab assignment with Single-A San Jose on May 13.
Pitched in an Arizona League game on May 13.
Had his rehab assignment transferred to Triple-A Sacramento on May 13.
Continuing his swing progression and increasing his baseball activity this week.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Expected to begin rehab assignment soon, potentially during the weekend of May 15-17, per Cardinals president of baseball operations Chaim Bloom. Eligible to come off injured list May 24.
Manager Oliver Marmol described that Urías has dealt with elbow discomfort on and off dating back to spring. Team will shut him down for multiple weeks before re-evaluating.
Pitched a scoreless inning vs. Indianapolis on May 3 as he continues a rehab assignment with Triple-A Memphis.

139.status: Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury, had his rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle on April 11 then shut down May 12 due to inflammation in his left shoulder. Will return to the Tampa Bay area for more imaging. No timeline for return.

A reliever for Triple-A Durham with a spot on the Rays' 40-man roster, Cook was placed on the Minor League IL on May 11. Had a 5.84 ERA with 15 strikeouts and six walks in 12 1/3 innings over 11 outings for Durham.

Felt soreness in the sixth inning of his May 3 start, then underwent an MRI on May 4 that revealed inflammation. Imaging showed no structural issues, so he should resume throwing after a brief period of rest.
Signed to a Major League contract on April 14 and immediately placed on the IL. Scheduled to face hitters for the first time the weekend of May 8-10, with a rehab assignment to follow.

Imaging revealed no structural damage. Faced hitters in live BP April 30, threw a bullpen session May 3 and departed for Triple-A Durham to begin a rehab assignment. Will need time to get built up as a starter.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Will need time to build up after being shut down so long.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

After rocky rehab outing on April 15, he reported soreness in back of right shoulder. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Shut down for 2-3 weeks, meaning he's likely 4-6 weeks from returning.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July.

140.status: Threw a bullpen on May 13 after being scratched from May 11 start against the D-backs. Rangers are optimistic he will make his next start.
Had a flare-up in his wrist while working back from his original injury. He’ll be working back from both “wrist inflammation” and the glute strain in the coming days.
Had a setback in his throwing program, but began throwing from 60 feet during the Rangers’ most recent homestand and has been cleared to begin his progression again.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.
Langford flew from Arizona to join the team in Detroit on May 2, and will not begin ramping up his swing for at least a week, focusing on cardio work and conditioning until cleared to hit.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Only threw one inning in an April 14 loss to the A’s due to arm fatigue that was eventually classified as shoulder impingement.
Left Tuesday night’s loss to the A’s after throwing two pitches due to feeling something in his arm. Scans showed minimal issues, though he will be down for at least two weeks.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Visited with doctor on May 12 after inflammation and soreness in his elbow. The Blue Jays expect to know more by the May 15 series opener in Detroit.
Expected to throw another bullpen session before his next rehab appearance is scheduled.
Has started hitting off a tee and light catching, which keeps him on track to potentially return by the end of the month.
Placed on the injured list after an MRI exam revealed a joint issue in his right elbow. He received a cortisone shot on May 11 and will pause baseball activity for 3-4 days.

141.injury: Right forearm tendinitis and left ankle inflammation
141.status: Received cortisone injections to his forearm on May 7 and his right thumb on May 11 to reduce soreness in both areas.
Scheduled to throw another live BP on May 12.
Will continue running the bases as he nears a rehab assignment with Low-A Dunedin on May 15-16.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Discovered after Estrada was optioned, so he has been placed on the Major League IL.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Threw May 12 and May 13, second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister’s MRI showed no muscle strains or tears, just inflammation. Aiming for a bullpen over the weekend.
Played catch on May 12 and May 13, aiming to build up over next week.
Was reviewed by Dr. Keith Meister, agreed with previous evaluations that only issue is from posterior elbow impingement. No forearm or UCL concerns. Started playing light catch on Wednesday.
Taking light swings and aiming for throwing program May 18. Will have a three-week build-up before a rehab assignment is scheduled.
Had stem cell injection in right shoulder following a rehab setback, will be no throw for three weeks.
Threw a live BP May 13, will see how he recovers May 14, before making plans on rehab assignment.

142.injury: Grade 2 AC joint sprain in left shoulder
142.status: Reporting improvements daily, could resume light baseball activity in a week, pending how each day goes.
Continues to report improvements in muscle strain, but will not resume baseball activities until muscle strain is fully cleared out.
Began throwing last week as far as 105 feet, and could be throwing on a mound again for evaluation by May 17.
Threw bullpen session on May 6 and is expected to take his turn as scheduled on May 9 in Cleveland.
Placed on IL on April 28. Last pitched on April 25, allowing one run and striking out two.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.More >

143.status: Played catch on May 12 for the first time since going on the IL. Will continue throwing program as he works toward a return.
Initially felt something in his calf while doing agility work on April 14. Began a rehab assignment on May 9, and continues to progress.
On a rehab assignment since April 30. Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique.

Miller was fielding ground balls in April but is no longer doing baseball activities. It might be a setback, although some say the Phillies are being cautious with his recovery. His progress has been slow, and the Phillies have declined to delve into details about it.

144.status: The outfielder is progressing well, but he won’t be activated before the homestand concludes on May 17.
He’ll likely be activated when he becomes eligible on May 18. Head slammed into the outfield wall while making a catch on May 10 at Dodger Stadium. Placed on 7-day concussion IL on May 11.

Began throwing bullpens in early May. A timetable hasn’t been set, but he’s not expected to be ready until late June or early July. Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow.

Received an encouraging update when he met with doctors in early May. But it’s too early to know when he might be cleared to begin throwing. Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18.
Expected to miss at least eight weeks. Was called for catcher's interference when Hyeseong Kim's bat hit his glove during May 10 game at Dodger Stadium.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Began an injury rehab assignment with the Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13.
Started injury rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte with a home run in game on May 12. Told Charlotte reporter Kendall Smith he felt 100 percent and ready to go pregame on May 13.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.
Hays had just come back from an IL stint due to a Grade 1 right hamstring strain before first feeling this injury on the off-day of April 30.
The White Sox Opening Day starter left his last start for Triple-A Charlotte on April 30 due to the shoulder issue. It’s the first time Smith has dealt with this malady this season.
Manager Will Venable wasn’t sure when Pereira suffered the injury during an April 28 win over the Angels. But it was bothering Pereira both hitting and throwing.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.playerName: Robby Snelling (Marlins No. 2 prospect, No. 28 overall)
146.status: Scratched from a start in Minnesota after reporting soreness during a bullpen session May 12. Initial MRI revealed ligament sprain.

146.playerName: Kevin Defrank (Marlins No. 5 prospect, No. 98 overall)
146.status: No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

According to manager Clayton McCullough, Conine is running at 70 percent, is hitting and throwing. Trending toward a quicker timeline of return. Underwent left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Underwent testing May 11 to gauge healing. Manager Aaron Boone said imaging showed the injury "still lingering there. Not any worse. He's upped what he's been able to do. But I don't think it allows us to clear him to really start ramping up the running yet."
Sustained injury May 10 at Milwaukee. Underwent MRI May 12 in New York. Manager Aaron Boone suggested Caballero could miss the minimum 10 days. Caballero vowed to be ready May 22.

Made fifth Minor League rehab start May 10 for Double-A Somerset, striking out eight over five innings. Still “probably a few [starts] away” before activation, manager Aaron Boone said May 11. Next start is likely May 16.
Injured shoulder while crashing into wall making a catch May 7. Received PRP injection May 11. Manager Aaron Boone indicated it could be "a few weeks" before return to play.
Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Will not throw for at least three weeks.

Will not throw for approximately three weeks. Manager Aaron Boone said there is “acute and chronic stuff going on there” that will require rest. Experienced difficulty while throwing on April 25 in Houston.
Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

158.status: Experienced leg cramping for the second time in the span of three starts on May 13 against the Padres, but expects to make his next start on schedule. (last updated: May 13)
Returned from IL on May 12 against Padres but out of lineup May 13 as precaution with back tightness. "He wants to play," said Pat Murphy, who vetoed desire out of caution. (last updated: May 13)

Threw a 50-pitch bullpen session on May 11 and is scheduled to restart a rehab assignment on May 16. Tentatively, his first start will be for High-A Wisconsin against South Bend. The Brewers' new estimate for a return date is early June. (last updated: May 12)

Scheduled to throw a bullpen session on May 14, which would be his first mound work since exiting an April 30 start complaining of a "dead arm" sensation. A return before the end of May remains possible. (last updated: May 12)

Slid into a concrete section of the wall while chasing a foul ball on May 8 against the Yankees. X-rays were negative, but he suffered a deep laceration that will require at least a month to heal. (last updated: May 12)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 12)
Completed live batting practice on May 11 and traveled to Triple-A Nashville, where he is scheduled to begin a rehab assignment on May 13. (last updated: May 12)
Scheduled to begin a rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville on May 15. (last updated: May 12)
Successfully threw a bullpen session on May 12 for the first time since going down with an elbow injury. (last updated: May 12)
